title: ulmus campestris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Ulmus campestris subsp. vulgaris, also known as the Wych Elm, is a subspecies of the Ulmus campestris tree, native to Europe and Western Asia. This deciduous tree has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Wych Elm include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions, and act as an antiseptic. The herbal preparations of Wych Elm include bark and leaves infusions, as well as tinctures and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally under medical supervision.
